description: Trigger discrimination for the operator CONSOLE / action surface (this skill) vs the authorization POLICY it enforces (authorization-matrix-designer), operator TELEMETRY (observability-operator), AI-AGENT authority (agent-authorization-matrix), and the incident PLAYBOOK the console serves (incident-response-runbook).

cases.prompt: Design the internal admin console: least-privilege operator tiers, audited cross-tenant access, marked and time-boxed impersonation, break-glass elevation, and a control-plane for retry/failover/data-repair.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — tiers, audited cross-tenant action, impersonation mechanics, break-glass, and control-plane actions are the console that operators ACT through. Not policy authoring, not telemetry, not a playbook.
cases.reason: The operator action surface and its controls are this skill; it enforces the authorization policy and serves the incident runbook rather than being either.

cases.prompt: Define who can do what: platform vs tenant roles, object-level permissions per resource, deny-by-default, and the rules governing when support access and impersonation are allowed.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — authoring the roles × permissions × resources model and the impersonation/support RULES is policy. The console enforces those rules; it does not decide them.
cases.reason: The authorization policy (including impersonation rules) is authorization-matrix-designer. admin-console-architect designs the console that ENFORCES that policy at its action surface.

cases.prompt: Instrument the platform and build the operator dashboards: metrics, traces, logs, and health views so ops can SEE what the system is doing.
cases.reason: Telemetry and dashboards to SEE system state are observability-operator. The admin console is the ACTION surface; it may embed telemetry, but seeing is not acting.

cases.prompt: Define what our AI agents are allowed to do in the SDLC — which can merge, deploy, or change settings, and where a human must approve — the standing authority matrix for agents vs humans.
cases.reason: The standing authority of AI agents (merge/deploy/settings, human-approval gates) is agent-authorization-matrix. This console is for HUMAN operators; an agent must not be handed a human break-glass path.

cases.prompt: Write the incident response runbook: detection, severity classification, roles, communication, mitigation steps, and postmortem for a production outage.
cases.reason: The incident PLAYBOOK (steps/roles/comms during an incident) is incident-response-runbook. The console's tools (failover, data-repair) SERVE that playbook, but the runbook itself is a separate artifact.
